---
title: "EN615 Module 1"
author: "Dan RD"
date: "2026-05-24"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
library(ggplot2)
library(tinytex)
library(deSolve)
library(knitr)

```

## Fractal

Consider the "simple" non-autonomous IVP: 

$$
\frac{dy}{dt} = t - y^2,\quad y(0)=1
$$
Use Euler's method with $h=0.05$ and $t = 900$ to plot $t$ vs. $y$.

```{Eulers method}
## Use Euler's method to solve dy/dt = t - y^2, y(0) = 1 from t=0 to t = 900 and plot t vs y

h  <- 0.05
t0 <- 0
tf <- 900

# number of steps
N <- as.integer((tf - t0)/h)

# Allocate vectors
t <- numeric(N+1)
y <- numeric(N+1)

# Initial conditions
t[1] <- t0
y[1] <- 1

# Euler iteration 

for (n in 1:N) {
  t[n+1] <- t[n] + h
  y[n+1] <- y[n] + h * (t[n] - y[n]^2)
}

```


```{r euler-plot, echo = FALSE, fig.align='center', out.width='100%'}
# Use ggplot() to build your chart

h  <- 0.05
t0 <- 0
tf <- 900

# number of steps
N <- as.integer((tf - t0)/h)

# Allocate vectors
t <- numeric(N+1)
y <- numeric(N+1)

# Initial conditions
t[1] <- t0
y[1] <- 1

# Euler iteration 

for (n in 1:N) {
  t[n+1] <- t[n] + h
  y[n+1] <- y[n] + h * (t[n] - y[n]^2)
}

df <- data.frame(t = t, y = y)

ggplot(df, aes(t, y)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Euler: dy/dt = t - y^2,  y(0)=1  (h=0.05)",
    x = "t", y = "y"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "sans") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14)
  )
```


## Chaos

Consider the "simple" non-autonomous differential equation: 

$$
\frac{dy}{dt} = exp(t)\sin(y)
$$

a) sketch the graph of the solution that satisfies the IC $y(0) = 0.3$

```{r exp-plot, echo = FALSE, fig.align='center', out.width='100%'}
# --- (A) Build a direction field in (t, y) ---
t_range <- c(0, 4)              # adjust as desired
y_range <- c(0, 4)              # covers up to a bit above pi (~3.1416)

nt <- 25
ny <- 25

grid <- expand.grid(
  t = seq(t_range[1], t_range[2], length.out = nt),
  y = seq(y_range[1], y_range[2], length.out = ny)
)

# slope dy/dt
grid$slope <- exp(grid$t) * sin(grid$y)

# Convert slope field to short line segments:
# direction vector (dt, dy) = (1, slope), normalized for uniform segment length
L <- 0.18  # segment half-length; tweak for density/appearance

norm <- sqrt(1 + grid$slope^2)
grid$dt <- (1 / norm) * L
grid$dy <- (grid$slope / norm) * L

grid$t0 <- grid$t - grid$dt
grid$t1 <- grid$t + grid$dt
grid$y0 <- grid$y - grid$dy
grid$y1 <- grid$y + grid$dy

# --- (B) Numerically trace the solution through (0, 0.3) ---
f <- function(t, state, parms) {
  y <- state[1]
  list(exp(t) * sin(y))
}

times <- seq(t_range[1], t_range[2], by = 0.01)
sol <- ode(y = c(0.3), times = times, func = f, parms = NULL)
sol <- as.data.frame(sol)
names(sol) <- c("t", "y")

# --- (C) Plot ---
ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = grid,
    aes(x = t0, y = y0, xend = t1, yend = y1),
    linewidth = 0.25,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = sol,
    aes(x = t, y = y),
    linewidth = 1.0,
    color = "blue"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = pi, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = t_range, ylim = y_range) +
  labs(
    title = "Direction Field and Solution for y' = e^t sin(y),  y(0)=0.3",
    x = "t",
    y = "y"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )
```

b) Use the Range-Kutta method with step size $\Delta t = 0.3, \Delta t= 0.301$ and $\Delta t = .302$

```{RK4 Rk4-method}
f <- function(t, y) exp(t) * sin(y)

rk4_step <- function(t, y, h) {
  k1 <- f(t, y)
  k2 <- f(t + h/2, y + h*k1/2)
  k3 <- f(t + h/2, y + h*k2/2)
  k4 <- f(t + h,   y + h*k3)
  y + (h/6) * (k1 + 2*k2 + 2*k3 + k4)
}

# -----------------------------
# Time grid: 0, 0.3, ..., 9.9, 10.0
# -----------------------------
times <- c(seq(0, 9.9, by = 0.3), 10)

# -----------------------------
# Function to run RK4 over 'times'
# -----------------------------
rk4_path <- function(y0, times) {
  y <- numeric(length(times))
  y[1] <- y0
  for (i in 1:(length(times) - 1)) {
    t <- times[i]
    h <- times[i + 1] - times[i]   # 0.3 except last step 0.1
    y[i + 1] <- rk4_step(t, y[i], h)
  }
  y
}

# -----------------------------
# Compute 3 paths for 3 initial conditions
# -----------------------------
y_0300 <- rk4_path(0.300, times)
y_0301 <- rk4_path(0.301, times)
y_0302 <- rk4_path(0.302, times)

# -----------------------------
# Data frame with time + 3 columns
# -----------------------------
df <- data.frame(
  time   = times,
  y_0300 = y_0300,
  y_0301 = y_0301,
  y_0302 = y_0302
)

```

```{r, echo= FALSE, results='asis'}

f <- function(t, y) exp(t) * sin(y)

rk4_step <- function(t, y, h) {
  k1 <- f(t, y)
  k2 <- f(t + h/2, y + h*k1/2)
  k3 <- f(t + h/2, y + h*k2/2)
  k4 <- f(t + h,   y + h*k3)
  y + (h/6) * (k1 + 2*k2 + 2*k3 + k4)
}

# -----------------------------
# Time grid: 0, 0.3, ..., 9.9, 10.0
# -----------------------------
times <- c(seq(0, 9.9, by = 0.3), 10)

# -----------------------------
# Function to run RK4 over 'times'
# -----------------------------
rk4_path <- function(y0, times) {
  y <- numeric(length(times))
  y[1] <- y0
  for (i in 1:(length(times) - 1)) {
    t <- times[i]
    h <- times[i + 1] - times[i]   # 0.3 except last step 0.1
    y[i + 1] <- rk4_step(t, y[i], h)
  }
  y
}

# -----------------------------
# Compute 3 paths for 3 initial conditions
# -----------------------------
y_0300 <- rk4_path(0.300, times)
y_0301 <- rk4_path(0.301, times)
y_0302 <- rk4_path(0.302, times)

# -----------------------------
# Data frame with time + 3 columns
# -----------------------------
df <- data.frame(
  time   = times,
  y_0300 = y_0300,
  y_0301 = y_0301,
  y_0302 = y_0302
)

knitr::kable(df)

```

```{r rk4-plot, echo = FALSE, fig.align='center', out.width='100%'}
f <- function(t, y) exp(t) * sin(y)

rk4_step <- function(t, y, h) {
  k1 <- f(t, y)
  k2 <- f(t + h/2, y + h*k1/2)
  k3 <- f(t + h/2, y + h*k2/2)
  k4 <- f(t + h,   y + h*k3)
  y + (h/6) * (k1 + 2*k2 + 2*k3 + k4)
}

# -----------------------------
# Time grid: 0, 0.3, ..., 9.9, 10.0
# -----------------------------
times <- c(seq(0, 9.9, by = 0.3), 10)

# -----------------------------
# Function to run RK4 over 'times'
# -----------------------------
rk4_path <- function(y0, times) {
  y <- numeric(length(times))
  y[1] <- y0
  for (i in 1:(length(times) - 1)) {
    t <- times[i]
    h <- times[i + 1] - times[i]   # 0.3 except last step 0.1
    y[i + 1] <- rk4_step(t, y[i], h)
  }
  y
}

# -----------------------------
# Compute 3 paths for 3 initial conditions
# -----------------------------
y_0300 <- rk4_path(0.300, times)
y_0301 <- rk4_path(0.301, times)
y_0302 <- rk4_path(0.302, times)

# -----------------------------
# Data frame with time + 3 columns
# -----------------------------
df <- data.frame(
  time   = times,
  y_0300 = y_0300,
  y_0301 = y_0301,
  y_0302 = y_0302
)


# Convert to long format without extra packages:
df_long <- rbind(
  data.frame(time = df$time, y = df$y_0300, IC = "y(0)=0.300"),
  data.frame(time = df$time, y = df$y_0301, IC = "y(0)=0.301"),
  data.frame(time = df$time, y = df$y_0302, IC = "y(0)=0.302")
)

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = time, y = y, color = IC)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "RK4: y' = exp(t) * sin(y) (h=0.3; last step adjusted to reach t=10)",
    x = "Time t",
    y = "y(t)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

```
